originYear.label: Year of Origin
originYear.rangeDescription: This piece was made between {{min}} and {{max}}.
originYear.fromDescription: This piece was made from {{year}} onwards.
originYear.unknownDescription: The year of manufacture of this piece is unknown.

authenticity.label: Authenticity
authenticity.originalDescription: A verified original from the stated period.
authenticity.later_copyDescription: A historical copy made later than the original.
authenticity.reproductionDescription: A modern replica without historical value.
authenticity.questionableDescription: The authenticity of this piece is disputed or cannot be clearly confirmed.
authenticity.unknownDescription: The authenticity has not yet been verified.

condition.label: Condition
condition.excellentDescription: Nearly perfect condition without significant signs of wear.
condition.greatDescription: Very well preserved with only minor signs of aging.
condition.goodDescription: Well preserved with visible but acceptable signs of wear.
condition.fairDescription: Noticeable signs of wear present, but still complete and stable.
condition.unknownDescription: The condition has not been assessed.

provenance.label: Provenance
provenance.completeDescription: The provenance is fully documented from creation to present day.
provenance.partialDescription: The provenance is partially documented but has gaps.
provenance.claimedDescription: The seller states a provenance, but it is not supported by documents.
provenance.unknownDescription: The provenance could not yet be determined.

restoration.label: Restoration
restoration.noneDescription: No restoration performed, the piece is in original condition.
restoration.minorDescription: Light restoration work such as cleaning or small repairs.
restoration.unknownDescription: No information about restoration is available.

error.description: Similar items could not be loaded.

productState.listedDescription: This item is visible but is not yet available for purchase.
productState.availableDescription: This item is available for purchase.
productState.reservedDescription: This item is currently reserved. It may become available again.

productState.removedDescription: This item was removed by the seller and can no longer be tracked.
productState.unknownDescription: The current availability of this item is unclear.

discover.title: We bring transparency into the opaque landscape of online antiques
discover.p1: We search through the vast offerings of thousands of scattered antique dealers, auction houses and marketplaces worldwide every day and bundle their items in one central place.
discover.p2: We not only capture current and upcoming items but also track price changes and availability. This way, you never miss a bargain and always keep an eye on the market.

highlight1.title: Over 500 Dealers, Auction Houses & Marketplaces
highlight1.description: We continuously search renowned antique dealers, auction houses and marketplaces from all over Europe and beyond.

highlight2.title: Near Real-Time Monitoring
highlight2.description: Newly offered antiques as well as price- or availability-changes are automatically captured in near real-time.

highlight3.title: Fully Language-agnostic
highlight3.description: We translate every antique into multiple languages. The language in which you search does not matter. We currently support English, German, French, Spanish and Italian.

landingPage.titleFirstLine: Discover rare antiques<1/>across the global market
landingPage.subtitle: Aura Historia searches antique dealers, auction houses, and marketplaces worldwide — helping you uncover pieces others never see.

periods.title: Browse historical eras
periods.description: Navigate time through collections of the world's most significant design movements.

features.title: Antiques are scattered everywhere online - your search shouldn't be
features.subtitle: We are the most sophisticated search engine for antiques and provide you with all the tools to efficiently search the antiques market.

search.title: Intelligent Search
search.description: Search hundreds of thousands of items with our powerful search engine. Cut out all the noise by filtering for condition, price, dealer, and much more.

global.title: Worldwide Coverage
global.description: We connect you with renowned antique dealers from over 15 countries – from Germany to the USA. No matter how small the dealer, if he has an online-shop, we are adding it.

history.title: Change History
history.description: Track all changes to an item – from price adjustments to status changes. We opt for the maximum amount of transparency.

watchlist.title: Personal Watchlist
watchlist.description: Save interesting items and keep track of their development. Get notified when price or availability changes.

translations.title: Automatic Translations
translations.description: Search and discover all items in your preferred language, without any barriers. We currently support English, German, French, Spanish and Italian.

personalFilter.title: Search Requests
personalFilter.description: Save your search request and get notified about new offers or updated items. The antiques you used to search for, now come to you.

aiSearchAgent.title: Personal AI Search Agent
aiSearchAgent.description: Your personal AI agent helps you find the most specific antiques. In combination with search requests, describe what you're looking for in plain language - as detailed as you wish. We will notify you as soon as hits match what you described.

communityWishes.title: Your wishes
communityWishes.description: You have critique, are missing something or have an idea what we should improve or add? We'll listen very closely. Contact us at contact@aura-historia.com and we will make it happen.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.subtitle: Just a few steps to your dream pieces.

step1.title: Search
step1.description: Enter what you're looking for – whether furniture, jewelry, art, or collectibles.

step2.title: Discover
step2.description: Browse results from thousands of dealers at a glance.

step3.title: Save
step3.description: Save interesting items to your watchlist or save your search criteria as an alert.

step4.title: Get notified
step4.description: Get notified by email as soon as an item on your watchlist changes or a new one matches your criteria.

testimonials.title: What our users say
testimonials.subtitle: Learn how we help collectors live their passion.

testimonial1.name: Margarete W.
testimonial1.quote: Finally a platform that makes searching for rare pieces easier. The price notifications have helped me find real bargains multiple times.

testimonial2.name: Heinrich K.
testimonial2.quote: As a dealer, I use Aura Historia to keep an eye on the market. The price developments help me enormously with my own pricing.

testimonial3.name: Elisabeth M.
testimonial3.quote: I'm amazed by the clarity. Before, I spent hours searching through different dealer sites. Now I find everything in one place.

newsletter.title: Stay informed
newsletter.description: Receive weekly curated finds, market trends, and exclusive insights directly in your inbox.
newsletter.privacy: By subscribing, you agree to our privacy policy. You can unsubscribe at any time.
newsletter.successMessage: Thank you for subscribing! Please confirm your email address.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Here you'll find answers to the most important questions about Aura Historia.

howItWorks.question: How does Aura Historia work?
howItWorks.answer: Aura Historia continuously searches the websites of over 500 antique dealers, auction houses and marketplaces worldwide and collects all offers in one central place. Our intelligent search allows you to find exactly what you're looking for.

directPurchase.question: Can I buy items directly through Aura Historia?
directPurchase.answer: No, Aura Historia is a pure search platform. If you want to buy an item, you will be redirected directly to the respective dealer's website where you can complete the purchase.

priceAlerts.question: How do price and availability notifications work?
priceAlerts.answer: Once you add an item to your watchlist, we automatically track its price- and availability development for you. As soon as something changes with your item, you receive a notification via E-Mail.

updateFrequency.question: How often is the data updated?
updateFrequency.answer: Our data is continuously updated. We operate in near real-time.

dealers.question: Which Antique Online-Shops are on Aura Historia?
dealers.answer: We include data from over 500 renowned antique dealers from more than 15 countries. Among them you'll find both, large auction houses and specialized dealers for all antique areas.

dealerRequest.question: I know an online dealer/auction house/marketplace that's not yet on Aura Historia. Can we add it?
dealerRequest.answer: Yes! Send us an email to contact@aura-historia.com and include the URL/Link of the shop along any additional information you might want to provide. We will verify the shop and include it within hours.

comparisonBarnebys.question: How does Aura Historia compare to Barnebys?
comparisonBarnebys.answer: While Barnebys is also an antiques search engine, they only focus on the largest players - the big auction houses and dealers. This makes up for a very high chance for you to miss out on what you're actually searching for. Ask yourself, where did I find my biggest treasures? It probably was a small local dealer or even thrift store.

comparisonPriceDatabase.question: How does Aura Historia compare to price databases like WorthPoint and the salesroom?
comparisonPriceDatabase.answer: Price databases leverage the sheer amount of historical data with auction results dating back to the 90s. While this helps finding comparisons for astronomically rare pieces, it often deflects the current market-value and availability of antiques. If your goal is to find antiques or research their current value, Aura Historia is the right place. We also want to take a heavy standpoint against the monetization of historical antique pricing data. You should not need to pay to find out what sold at which price.

pricing.title: Choose Your Plan
pricing.subtitle: From casual collectors to professional dealers — we have the right plan for you.

descriptions.default: Explore carefully selected objects from this category, each documented with provenance clues, craftsmanship details, and current market visibility.
descriptions.ANCIENT_POTTERY: Vessels, votive pieces, and ceramic fragments that reveal the rituals, trade routes, and daily life of ancient civilizations.
descriptions.STORAGE_CONTAINERS: Collectors searching for antique trunks for sale, vintage wooden chests, and historical storage boxes gain an intriguing look at how past generations secured their belongings. Visitors can find massive brass-bound steamer trunks, intricately carved blanket chests, and delicate inlaid wooden tea caddies. The selection frequently includes vintage apothecary drawer sets, antique leather hat boxes, and early tin advertising canisters. From heavy oak document boxes with complex lock mechanisms to woven sweetgrass baskets, those looking to buy antique storage containers will find items highlighting historical joinery and the utilitarian necessities of daily domestic life.
descriptions.LIGHTING_OBJECTS: Collectors searching for antique lighting fixtures, vintage chandeliers for sale, and historical lamps will discover a wide spectrum of functional and decorative illumination from various centuries. The offerings range from early pewter whale oil lamps and brass candlestick holders to elaborate Victorian gasoliers and authentic Art Nouveau slag glass table lamps. Enthusiasts looking to buy antique ceiling lights can also find crystal prism chandeliers, industrial enamel factory pendants, and early electric desk lamps with original wiring mechanisms. These pieces provide insight into the technological progression of lighting, historical glassmaking, and domestic interior design.
descriptions.BOOKS_MANUSCRIPTS_PRINTED_MEDIA: Bibliophiles searching for rare first-edition books, antique manuscripts for sale, and early printed materials get a rich selection of historical documents. Visitors looking to buy antique books online can expect leather-bound literary classics, illuminated manuscripts with intricate calligraphy, and collectible historical maps. The category also houses original lithographs, early copperplate engravings, and rare religious texts. Whether seeking a signed nineteenth-century novel or evaluating antique book prices for a centuries-old scientific treatise, collectors will encounter items showcasing traditional bookbinding, typography, and historical printing techniques.
descriptions.FURNITURE: Interior historians and collectors searching for authentic antique furniture for sale, vintage wooden furniture, and specific period pieces can explore a diverse array of domestic items from Georgian, Victorian, Art Deco, and mid-century modern eras. Discover solid mahogany dining tables, intricately carved oak cabinets, and elegant walnut writing desks featuring dovetail joinery. The selection often includes tufted leather armchairs, antique marquetry sideboards, and early painted folk furniture. Those looking to buy antique furniture online will find pieces that reflect historical woodworking techniques, period-specific upholstery, and the evolving aesthetic tastes of past domestic life.
descriptions.TOYS_GAMES_RECREATIONAL: Toy collectors searching for antique toys for sale, vintage board games, and early mechanical playthings can explore a fascinating assortment of historical childhood entertainment. The selection frequently features cast iron mechanical banks, lithographed tin wind-up toys, and authentic antique porcelain dolls in period clothing. Enthusiasts looking to buy vintage wooden toys or Victorian parlor games can also discover early rocking horses, complex moving dioramas, and complete sets of historical board games. These items reveal specific manufacturing materials, mechanical ingenuity, and the broader cultural significance of historical childhood and traditional amusement.
descriptions.TEXTILES: Textile historians searching for antique textiles for sale, vintage tapestries, and historical clothing will discover a vibrant array of early fabrics, garments, and woven goods. The offerings often include handmade patchwork quilts, intricate antique Flemish tapestries, and delicate lace collars from the Victorian era. Enthusiasts looking to buy vintage rugs or historical samplers might also find preserved silk kimonos, early woven Navajo rugs, and traditional embroidered textiles detailing family histories. These preserved fabrics showcase the evolution of early natural dyes, historical looming techniques, hand-spinning processes, and the cultural significance of garment construction.
descriptions.CLOCKS_TIMEKEEPING: Horology enthusiasts searching for antique clocks for sale, vintage pocket watches, and rare historical timepieces will find an exquisite assortment of mechanical timekeepers. The category encompasses authentic grandfather clocks, ornate French mantel clocks in gilded bronze, and precision marine chronometers used for early navigation. Collectors looking to buy vintage pocket watches can discover enameled silver cases, complex repeating movements, and early fusee mechanisms. From Black Forest cuckoo clocks to sophisticated carriage clocks, the available items highlight the intricate gears, escapements, and decorative artistry that define the historical evolution of measuring time.
descriptions.JEWELRY_PERSONAL_ADORNMENT: Jewelry historians and buyers searching for antique jewelry for sale, vintage rings, and authentic estate jewelry will find an exquisite array of personal adornments spanning the Georgian, Victorian, Art Nouveau, and Art Deco periods. Discover intricate cameo brooches, authentic mourning jewelry, and vintage platinum engagement rings set with old mine-cut diamonds. The selection frequently includes antique enameled pocket watch chains, sterling silver chatelaines, and faceted garnet necklaces. These wearable artifacts highlight historical gemstone cutting, the evolution of precious metalwork, and the shifting fashions of past societies for collectors of rare jewelry.
descriptions.ARCHITECTURAL_ELEMENTS: Preservationists searching for architectural antiques for sale, reclaimed building materials, and salvaged structural elements will find an extensive array of components from historically significant structures. The offerings include massive hand-hewn oak ceiling beams, authentic Victorian stained glass windows, and heavy cast iron entry gates. Collectors looking to buy antique fireplace mantels or vintage door hardware can discover ornate marble surrounds, terracotta roof tiles, and brass fixtures with original patinas. These structural pieces allow for the study and reuse of traditional building techniques, historical masonry, and the architectural heritage of the built environment.
descriptions.ARCHAEOLOGICAL_ARTIFACTS: Antiquities collectors searching for genuine archaeological artifacts for sale, ancient pottery, and historical relics will discover a profound selection of items recovered from historical digs around the world. The category frequently features ancient Roman glass vessels, authentic Egyptian ushabti figures, and Neolithic knapped flint arrowheads. Enthusiasts looking to buy ancient coins or Pre-Columbian artifacts can also find terracotta pottery, ancient Greek bronze coins, and early Mesopotamian cuneiform tablets. These excavated items provide a direct, tangible connection to human prehistory, offering invaluable insights into ancient daily life and the material culture of vanished civilizations.
descriptions.VISUAL_ART: Art historians and collectors searching for antique paintings for sale, original historical artwork, and vintage sculptures can browse a meticulously cataloged archive of visual pieces spanning numerous artistic movements. Discover eighteenth-century classical oil portraits, detailed botanical watercolors, and authentic early bronze figurative sculptures. The category frequently includes options for those looking to buy antique landscape paintings, Renaissance-era graphite sketches, and traditional Japanese woodblock prints. From monumental academy paintings to intimate miniature portraits, these works support the study of traditional artistic techniques, historical pigments, and the evolution of human visual expression.
descriptions.DECORATIVE_OBJECTS: Collectors searching for decorative antiques for sale, vintage home decor, and historical ornamental objects will discover a broad spectrum of items designed to elevate domestic spaces across various centuries. The offerings range from fine antique Sevres porcelain vases and intricate Meissen figurines to elegant Art Deco bronze bookends and Victorian majolica serving pieces. Enthusiasts looking to buy antique mirrors or vintage art glass can find ornate gilded frames, early Venetian glassware, and classical marble busts. These items document the evolution of decorative art movements, domestic ornamentation, and the meticulous craftsmanship of historical artisans.
descriptions.EPHEMERA_MEMORABILIA: Archival collectors searching for historical ephemera for sale, vintage paper memorabilia, and antique documents will find a fascinating assortment of preserved paper goods capturing daily historical life. The selection includes original handwritten Civil War correspondence, colorful Victorian trade cards, and early lithographed advertising posters. Enthusiasts looking to buy vintage postcards, authentic historical tickets, or old theatrical playbills can discover extensive collections of early photographic postcards and world fair memorabilia. These primary source materials provide a unique window into past commercial advertising, political movements, and significant cultural events.
descriptions.PHOTOGRAPHY_OPTICAL_MEDIA: Enthusiasts searching for vintage cameras for sale, antique photography, and historical optical devices will find a comprehensive selection of early image-capturing equipment. The offerings include cased daguerreotype portraits, original early wooden bellows cameras, and intricate brass magic lanterns with hand-painted glass slides. Collectors looking to buy antique stereoview cards, early tintypes, and large-format glass plate negatives will discover extensive archives of optical media. These items document the chemistry of early photo processing, the technological evolution of the camera, and the societal impact of early visual documentation.
descriptions.MUSICAL_INSTRUMENTS: Musicologists and buyers searching for antique musical instruments for sale, vintage acoustic guitars, and historical brass instruments can explore a rich variety of early acoustic devices. The selection frequently features rare gut-string violins, ornate wooden flutes, and early brass instruments such as keyed bugles and rotary-valve cornets. Enthusiasts looking to purchase antique pianos or traditional ethnographic percussion instruments will also find beautifully restored grand pianos and antique concertinas. These historical items showcase the evolution of acoustic engineering, traditional luthier craftsmanship, and the historical materials used to create musical sound.
descriptions.WEAPONS: Collectors searching for authentic antique swords, vintage firearms for sale, and historical armor will discover a vast array of edged weapons and early firearms from various global conflicts. The selection frequently features Renaissance rapiers, medieval broadswords, and original Japanese katana blades, alongside operational flintlock pistols, antique percussion-cap muskets, and ornate powder flasks. Enthusiasts looking to buy antique military defense items can also find authentic chainmail garments, ceremonial plate armor, and decorative shields, offering a deep dive into historical metallurgy, combat tactics, and the craftsmanship of early warfare implements.
descriptions.TOOLS_IMPLEMENTS: Collectors searching for antique hand tools for sale, vintage woodworking tools, and historical farm implements will find a robust selection of devices used by historical tradespeople before widespread mechanization. Discover beautifully patinated wooden molding planes, heavy forged-iron broadaxes, and precision brass calipers used by early engineers. The category often includes specialized antique tools for cooperage, leatherworking, and stonemasonry. For those looking to buy vintage blacksmith tools or early carpentry equipment, these functional artifacts reflect the physical labor of past centuries and the ingenuity of early hand-forged steel and traditional artisans.
descriptions.INDUSTRIAL_TRADE_EQUIPMENT: This category appeals to those searching for antique industrial tools, vintage factory equipment, and historical trade machinery for sale. Enthusiasts can uncover cast-iron textile looms, early printing presses, and heavy-duty agricultural machinery that powered the Industrial Revolution. The selection frequently includes specialized artisanal equipment for those looking to buy antique blacksmith anvils, vintage cobblers' benches, and early factory time clocks. Whether searching for a vintage drafting table or salvaged industrial molds, collectors will find rugged, functional artifacts that document the transformation of manufacturing, trade labor, and mass production.
descriptions.CULTURAL_ETHNOGRAPHIC: Anthropological collectors searching for authentic ethnographic art for sale, tribal artifacts, and indigenous cultural objects will find a diverse collection representing the material culture of global societies. The selection frequently features carved African ceremonial masks, intricate Polynesian navigational charts, and Native American beadwork. Enthusiasts looking to buy traditional cultural artifacts can also discover Tibetan prayer wheels, authentic South American textiles, and early Inuit bone carvings. These ethnographic objects offer profound insights into traditional belief systems, social structures, indigenous materials, and the highly localized craftsmanship of diverse historical communities.
descriptions.MILITARIA: Military historians searching for authentic militaria for sale, antique military uniforms, and historical war artifacts will discover a comprehensive array of items documenting global conflicts. The offerings frequently feature original World War I wool tunics, inscribed trench art, and early leather flight helmets. Enthusiasts looking to buy antique military gear or wartime collectibles can also find intricate military unit insignia, original wartime propaganda posters, and historical field optics such as brass trench periscopes. These items serve as vital educational tools for understanding military structures, battlefield logistics, and the impact of global warfare.
descriptions.COINS_CURRENCY_MEDALS: Numismatists searching for rare coins for sale, antique paper money, and historical military medals can explore a vast selection of global currency and commemorative medallions. Discover authentic silver denarii from the Roman Empire, early American colonial paper scrip, and intricate gold sovereigns. The category also supports those looking to buy vintage banknotes, obsolete bank notes, and rare mint errors. These financial artifacts provide a detailed look at monetary evolution, early metal minting processes, historical inflation, and the political iconography stamped on global currency throughout human history.
descriptions.NATURAL_HISTORY_CURIOSITIES: Collectors searching for antique natural history specimens, vintage taxidermy for sale, and historical oddities will find an intriguing array of items gathered from early biological exploration. The category features authentic Victorian taxidermy dioramas, intricate early anatomical papier-mache models, and preserved entomology displays. Enthusiasts looking to buy antique fossils, historical geological samples, or old botanical prints can discover fossilized ammonites, antique skeletal articulations, and historical herbarium folios. These fascinating items provide deep context for the history of scientific exploration, biological classification, and the Victorian fascination with the natural world.
descriptions.TRANSPORTATION_TRAVEL_OBJECTS: This category appeals to enthusiasts searching for antique travel artifacts, vintage luggage for sale, and early transportation memorabilia. Collectors can find polished brass maritime binnacles, early leather carriage trunks, and original railway lanterns used by nineteenth-century conductors. The selection often includes vintage leather motoring goggles, historical steamship passenger logs, and early navigational sextants. Whether looking to buy antique maritime equipment, a velocipede bicycle, or a detailed scale model galleon, these artifacts document the historical challenges and technological advancements of early passenger travel and global exploration.
descriptions.RELIGIOUS_RITUAL_CEREMONIAL: Researchers and collectors searching for antique religious artifacts for sale, historical ritual objects, and sacred ceremonial items will find an array of works representing global spiritual practices. The selection includes heavily embroidered clerical vestments, intricately carved wooden crucifixes, and authentic early Jewish silver Torah pointers. Enthusiasts looking to buy antique Buddhist artifacts, illuminated Islamic texts, or ornate brass thuribles can discover antique singing bowls, Quran folios, and incense burners. These pieces provide essential insight into comparative religion, historical worship practices, and the rich material culture associated with human spirituality.
descriptions.WRITING_COMMUNICATION_INSTRUMENTS: Collectors searching for antique writing instruments for sale, vintage fountain pens, and historical communication devices will discover an array of tools that shaped human correspondence. The selection frequently features heavy cast-iron telegraph keys, early mechanical typewriters with glass keys, and elegant sterling silver dip pens. Enthusiasts looking to buy antique inkwells, authentic wax seal stamps, and vintage rotary-dial telephones can find ornate crystal inkwells and beautifully restored communication equipment. These artifacts serve as a comprehensive guide to understanding the historical progression of the written word and early long-distance communication.
descriptions.SCIENTIFIC_MEDICAL_TECHNICAL: Collectors searching for antique scientific instruments for sale, vintage medical tools, and historical engineering devices will discover a highly specialized selection of empirical tools. Discover gleaming brass surveying theodolites, early achromatic microscopes in original mahogany cases, and complex antique calculating machines. The category frequently includes options for those looking to buy antique surgical kits, early apothecary scales, and intricate celestial globes. These historical devices chronicle the evolution of empirical science, historical astronomy, early navigation, and the development of healthcare practices before modern technology.

header.defaultDescription: Explore curated objects from this era and discover their historical context, material culture, and provenance clues.

descriptions.ANTIQUITY: Collectors searching for authentic Antiquity artifacts for sale, ancient Greek pottery, and Roman antiquities will discover a profound selection of items recovered from early civilizations. Our aggregated index compiles available ancient coins, Egyptian ushabti figures, and Neolithic stone tools from various specialized dealers. Enthusiasts looking to buy genuine ancient relics can find ancient bronze sculptures, early Mesopotamian artifacts, and classical Mediterranean antiquities. These excavated items provide a tangible connection to human prehistory and the foundational material culture of the ancient world.
descriptions.ART_DECO: Buyers searching for authentic Art Deco antiques for sale, vintage 1920s furniture, and geometric decor will find a glamorous array of items spanning the 1920s and 1930s. Our directory aggregates listings from multiple shops, featuring streamlined walnut sideboards, chrome lighting fixtures, and classic flapper-era jewelry. Collectors looking to buy original Art Deco art or vintage modernist sculptures can discover mirrored vanities, colorful Bakelite accessories, and bold architectural salvage that define this iconic period of luxury and early modern design.
descriptions.ART_NOUVEAU: Enthusiasts searching for Art Nouveau antiques for sale, original Belle Epoque decor, and vintage floral jewelry will discover an exquisite collection defined by organic lines and natural motifs. Our consolidated index features available authentic Tiffany style lamps, intricate silver vanity sets, and flowing mahogany furniture sourced from diverse dealers. Collectors looking to buy Art Nouveau art glass, original Mucha lithographs, or sinuous bronze sculptures can easily locate specific historical pieces that capture the romantic essence of turn-of-the-century craftsmanship.
descriptions.ARTS_AND_CRAFTS: Collectors searching for Arts and Crafts antiques for sale, authentic Mission style furniture, and vintage artisan decor will find a robust selection of items emphasizing handcraftsmanship. Our aggregated directory compiles solid quarter-sawn oak tables, hammered copper lighting fixtures, and original William Morris textiles from various specialized shops. Those looking to buy authentic Craftsman antiques, vintage matte green pottery, or early 20th-century artisan metalwork will discover pieces that rejected mass production in favor of natural materials and honest, functional design.
descriptions.BAROQUE: Buyers searching for authentic Baroque antiques for sale, ornate 17th-century furniture, and dramatic historical art will discover a magnificent array of highly detailed artifacts. Our index compiles available listings from multiple dealers, featuring elaborately carved walnut cabinets, gilded mirrors, and dramatic chiaroscuro oil paintings. Collectors looking to buy original Baroque decor, heavy brass chandeliers, or intricate tapestry textiles can easily locate these opulent, theatrical pieces that define the grandeur and intense emotion of 17th and early 18th-century European design.
descriptions.BIEDERMEIER: Enthusiasts searching for authentic Biedermeier furniture for sale, early 19th-century German antiques, and elegant vintage decor will find a sophisticated selection of restrained, functional items. Our consolidated directory aggregates light fruitwood cabinets, cleanly designed writing desks, and gently curved seating sourced from diverse antique shops. Collectors looking to buy original Biedermeier antiques, delicate domestic clocks, or refined parlor pieces will discover specific historical items that showcase the simplified elegance and practical beauty of Central European middle-class design.
descriptions.CONTEMPORARY: Collectors searching for Contemporary art for sale, modern studio crafts, and late 20th-century design pieces will discover a dynamic array of items reflecting modern artistic expression. Our aggregated index compiles available listings of studio glass, cutting-edge abstract paintings, and innovative sculptural furniture from various galleries and dealers. Those looking to buy contemporary artisan crafts, postmodern ceramics, or late-century mixed media works can easily locate unique, boundary-pushing pieces that represent the ongoing evolution of modern material culture and artistic innovation.
descriptions.EARLY_MEDIEVAL: Researchers and collectors searching for Early Medieval artifacts for sale, authentic Dark Ages relics, and Migration Period antiquities will find a rare selection of historical items. Our directory aggregates available authentic Viking jewelry, early Anglo-Saxon metalwork, and Merovingian pottery sourced from specialized antiquity dealers. Buyers looking to purchase authentic medieval weapons, early forged iron tools, or ancient fibula brooches will discover crucial historical artifacts that document the turbulent centuries following the fall of the Roman Empire and early European development.
descriptions.EARLY_MODERN: Collectors searching for Early Modern antiques for sale, Renaissance to Enlightenment era artifacts, and 16th to 18th-century decor will discover a transformative array of historical items. Our consolidated index compiles available early scientific instruments, Elizabethan oak furniture, and historical exploration maps from various specialized shops. Enthusiasts looking to buy original Early Modern art, antique leather-bound books, or early navigational tools can locate specific pieces that represent the profound global expansion and scientific awakening of the post-medieval world.
descriptions.EMPIRE: Buyers searching for authentic Empire style furniture for sale, Napoleonic era antiques, and neoclassical 19th-century decor will find a commanding selection of grand historical artifacts. Our aggregated directory features available mahogany console tables with ormolu mounts, classical bronze candelabras, and militaristic decorative objects sourced from multiple dealers. Collectors looking to buy original French Empire antiques, stately monumental clocks, or imperial-themed tapestries can easily locate these impressive pieces that reflect the authority, classical revival, and political grandeur of early 19th-century Europe.
descriptions.GOTHIC: Enthusiasts searching for authentic Gothic antiques for sale, medieval style furniture, and original Gothic Revival decor will discover a dramatic collection of items featuring pointed arches and intricate tracery. Our index compiles available heavily carved oak chests, wrought iron lighting, and medieval ecclesiastical fragments from diverse antique shops. Collectors looking to buy original Gothic art, authentic stained glass windows, or medieval-inspired architectural salvage can locate specific historical pieces that capture the soaring spirituality and complex craftsmanship of the period.
descriptions.HISTORICISM: Collectors searching for Historicism antiques for sale, authentic 19th-century revival furniture, and Victorian-era historical reproductions will find a rich array of eclectic, nostalgic items. Our aggregated directory compiles available Neo-Renaissance cabinets, Neo-Gothic seating, and elaborately decorated Victorian ceramics sourced from multiple dealers. Those looking to buy original Historicist decor, grand 19th-century bronze sculptures, or heavily ornamented parlor furnishings will discover pieces that represent the era's fascinating obsession with combining and reinventing the great design styles of the past.
descriptions.MANNERISM: Art historians and buyers searching for Mannerist antiques for sale, late Renaissance art, and 16th-century European decor will discover a sophisticated selection of items known for artificiality and elegance. Our consolidated index features available elongated bronze sculptures, complex allegorical paintings, and heavily ornamented Italianate furniture sourced from specialized shops. Collectors looking to buy original Mannerist art, elaborate metalwork, or late 16th-century tapestries can locate specific pieces that showcase the stylized exaggeration, intellectual complexity, and transitional design of this fascinating period.
descriptions.MID_CENTURY_MODERN: Enthusiasts searching for authentic Mid-Century Modern furniture for sale, vintage 1950s decor, and retro MCM lighting will find a highly sought-after collection of sleek, functional design. Our aggregated directory compiles available original teak sideboards, molded fiberglass chairs, and atomic era starburst clocks sourced from multiple vintage dealers. Collectors looking to buy original Danish Modern antiques, geometric retro artwork, or iconic mid-century lounge chairs can easily locate these clean-lined, innovative pieces that define the post-war domestic aesthetic.
descriptions.MODERNISM: Buyers searching for authentic Modernist antiques for sale, Bauhaus design pieces, and early 20th-century avant-garde decor will discover a revolutionary array of minimalist, form-follows-function items. Our index compiles available tubular steel furniture, early geometric abstract art, and purely functional industrial design objects from diverse specialized shops. Collectors looking to buy original Modernist decor, vintage constructivist posters, or early 20th-century architectural models will find pieces that radically broke from historical ornamentation to embrace the modern machine age.
descriptions.NEOCLASSICISM: Collectors searching for authentic Neoclassical antiques for sale, late 18th-century classical furniture, and elegant Georgian decor will find a refined selection of symmetrically balanced items. Our aggregated directory features available delicate mahogany demilune tables, classical marble busts, and silver urns featuring Greek key motifs sourced from multiple dealers. Those looking to buy original Neoclassical art, Louis XVI style seating, or Wedgwood jasperware can easily locate these orderly, graceful pieces that sought to revive the pure aesthetics of ancient Greece and Rome.
descriptions.POSTMODERN: Enthusiasts searching for Postmodern antiques for sale, vintage 1980s Memphis design, and late 20th-century eclectic decor will discover a bold, colorful collection of rebellious items. Our consolidated index compiles available asymmetrical furniture, vividly patterned laminate objects, and playful sculptural lighting sourced from diverse vintage shops. Collectors looking to buy original Postmodern art, ironic retro-modern design pieces, or unconventional studio ceramics can locate specific historical items that humorously challenged the strict rules of traditional modernist design.
descriptions.RENAISSANCE: Buyers searching for authentic Renaissance antiques for sale, 15th and 16th-century European artifacts, and early humanist art will discover a profoundly important array of cultural items. Our aggregated directory compiles available heavily carved Italian cassoni chests, early printed books, and classical bronze figurative sculptures sourced from specialized antiquity dealers. Collectors looking to buy original Renaissance art, early maiolica pottery, or authentic 16th-century tapestries can easily locate these magnificent pieces that mark the rebirth of classical learning and artistic perspective.
descriptions.ROCOCO: Collectors searching for authentic Rococo antiques for sale, 18th-century French furniture, and ornate Louis XV decor will find a lavish selection of asymmetrical, nature-inspired items. Our index compiles available gilded mirrors with scrolling acanthus leaves, delicately curved cabriole leg tables, and pastel romanticist paintings from multiple dealers. Those looking to buy original Rococo art, fine Sevres porcelain, or elaborate ormolu clocks will discover highly decorative pieces that define the playful, elegant, and lighthearted excess of the mid-18th century.
descriptions.ROMANESQUE: Researchers and buyers searching for authentic Romanesque antiques for sale, 11th and 12th-century medieval artifacts, and early architectural elements will discover a rare collection of robust, massive historical items. Our aggregated directory features available early stone sculptural fragments, heavy forged ironwork, and early medieval religious relics sourced from specialized antiquarians. Collectors looking to buy authentic Romanesque art, early Christian antiquities, or monumental medieval salvage can locate these sturdy, spiritually significant pieces characterized by thick walls and semi-circular arches.

consent.title: We value your privacy
consent.description: We use cookies to analyze web traffic and improve your user experience. By clicking 'Accept', you consent to our use of cookies for these purposes. You can find more information in our <1>privacy policy</1>.

consentSettings.title: Cookie Settings
consentSettings.description: We use cookies to analyze web traffic and improve your user experience. You can find more information in our <1>privacy policy</1>.
consentSettings.analyticsDescription: Allows us to analyze usage and improve your experience via Google Analytics. Includes ad storage, analytics storage, ad user data, and ad personalization.
consentSettings.saved: Your cookie preferences have been saved.

descriptions.default: Discover carefully curated objects from this collection with details on provenance, condition, and historical context. Our database aggregates offerings from specialized auction houses, dealers, and marketplaces, allowing you to compare a broad spectrum of authentic antiques in one place.
descriptions.biedermeier-furniture: Collectors looking for authentic Biedermeier furniture can discover elegant simplicity from the period of 1815 to 1848. Find cherry wood secretaires, walnut commodes, and Biedermeier tables from specialized auction houses and dealers. The Biedermeier era is defined by clean lines, fine woods, and deliberate restraint in decoration. On Aura Historia, you can compare furniture from this golden age of bourgeois domestic culture, offered by numerous European auction houses and specialist dealers.
descriptions.ancient-weapons: Those searching for ancient weapons can discover swords, spears, and armor from Greek, Roman, and Near Eastern origins. Find authentic militaria and edged weapons from specialized dealers. From Bronze Age daggers to Roman gladii and Etruscan helmets, each piece tells the story of conflicts and military technology from millennia past. Our platform aggregates offerings from leading auction houses and arms dealers to provide you with the widest selection.
descriptions.art-nouveau-jewelry: Lovers of Art Nouveau jewelry can find filigree brooches, enamel pendants, and organically shaped rings from the Belle Époque. Discover pieces by Lalique, Fouquet, and other Art Nouveau masters. Art Nouveau revolutionized jewelry with its flowing botanical forms, insect motifs, and the use of novel materials such as plique-à-jour enamel and opal glass. On Aura Historia, find exquisite pieces from specialized jewelers and international auction houses.
descriptions.art-deco-furniture: Buyers looking for Art Deco furniture can find geometric elegance from the 1920s and 1930s. Discover lacquered commodes, Macassar ebony tables, and chrome-accented furniture from specialized auction houses. Art Deco style combines luxurious materials with bold modernity — exotic woods, polished metal, and geometric patterns define the design. Compare a wide range of furniture from this iconic era across international sources on our platform.
descriptions.baroque-furniture: Collectors of Baroque furniture can find opulent cabinets, gilded console tables, and stately furniture from the 17th and 18th centuries. Discover authentic Baroque pieces from European specialists. The Baroque period produced lavish carvings, marquetry, and gilding that continue to captivate collectors today. From Italian cabinet-on-stands to Dutch hall cupboards, our platform provides access to a wide range of offerings from specialized dealers and auction houses.
descriptions.renaissance-paintings: Art lovers looking for Renaissance paintings can discover works from the Early Renaissance through Mannerism. Find panel paintings, portraits, and religious scenes from the 15th and 16th centuries. The Renaissance marks a golden age of European painting, with masters such as Botticelli, Titian, and Dürer. On Aura Historia, you can compare paintings from this era offered by leading art auction houses, galleries, and specialized art dealers worldwide.
descriptions.ancient-coins: Numismatists searching for ancient coins can find Greek drachmas, Roman denarii, and Byzantine solidi. Discover rare mintings and authentic coins from antiquity at specialized dealers. Ancient coins are unique testimonies of past cultures — they document rulers, deities, and historical events. Our platform brings together offerings from renowned coin auction houses and specialist dealers, helping you find the ideal addition to your collection.
descriptions.art-nouveau-lamps: Those looking for Art Nouveau lamps can discover Tiffany lights, Gallé vase lamps, and floral ceiling fixtures from the Art Nouveau era. Find authentic lighting objects circa 1900 from specialized dealers. Art Nouveau lighting combines artisanal perfection with organic forms — glass art, bronze, and enamel merge into stunning light sources. Compare lamps by Tiffany Studios, Gallé, Daum, and other renowned manufacturers from numerous specialized sources on Aura Historia.
descriptions.art-deco-jewelry: Lovers of Art Deco jewelry can find geometric brooches, diamond bracelets, and platinum rings from the 1920s and 1930s. Discover pieces by Cartier, Van Cleef & Arpels, and other masters. Art Deco jewelry combines geometric precision with luxurious gemstones and innovative settings. From cocktail rings to sautoirs and clip brooches, our platform lets you compare a wide range of this glamorous jewelry era from international auction houses.
descriptions.gothic-art: Collectors of Gothic art can discover altarpieces, sculptures, and panel paintings from the 12th to 15th centuries. Find authentic Gothic artworks and sacral objects from specialized dealers and auction houses. The Gothic period produced a unique fusion of spirituality and aesthetics — from delicate Madonnas to luminous stained glass and monumental carved altarpieces. On Aura Historia, compare Gothic artworks from various regions and periods and view offerings from specialized sources.
descriptions.rococo-furniture: Those looking for Rococo furniture can find curved commodes, bergères, and delicate writing desks from the 18th century. Discover the elegance of French Rococo from specialized antique dealers. Rococo is characterized by asymmetrical ornaments, shell motifs, and a playful lightness that succeeded the strict Baroque. From Louis XV commodes to Venetian console tables, Aura Historia provides access to a wide range of offerings from international dealers and auction houses.
descriptions.ancient-jewelry: Collectors of ancient jewelry can find gold chains, cameos, and fibulae from Greek, Roman, and Etruscan origins. Discover authentic jewelry pieces from antiquity at specialized auction houses. Ancient jewelry fascinates with its artisanal perfection and symbolic significance — from golden wreath motifs to engraved gemstones and filigree earrings. On our platform, you can compare objects from various cultures and periods of antiquity offered by renowned specialists.
descriptions.victorian-clocks: Clock enthusiasts looking for Victorian clocks can find grandfather clocks, pocket watches, and mantel clocks from the Historicism period. Discover timepieces from the 19th century by English and European manufacturers. The Victorian era produced a remarkable variety of clock types — from ornamental bracket clocks to regulators and skeleton clocks. On Aura Historia, compare historical clocks from this era and view offerings from leading clock auction houses and specialist shops.
descriptions.baroque-silver: Collectors of Baroque silver can find ceremonial goblets, table centerpieces, and candelabra from the 17th and 18th centuries. Discover gilded silver and masterworks by European silversmiths from specialized dealers. Baroque silver impresses with opulent repoussé work, rich decoration, and masterful gilding techniques. From Augsburg ceremonial tankards to London candelabra and Dutch salt cellars, our platform aggregates offerings from specialized silver auction houses and dealers.
descriptions.empire-furniture: Buyers searching for Empire furniture can find classical elegance from the Napoleonic era. Discover mahogany secretaires, pillar tables, and bronze-decorated commodes from specialized auction houses. Empire style combines ancient design language with imperial grandeur — Egyptian motifs, gilded bronze mounts, and strict symmetry define this era. On Aura Historia, compare Empire furniture from French, German, and Italian sources to find the perfect piece for your collection.
descriptions.biedermeier-clocks: Clock enthusiasts can find authentic Biedermeier clocks from the period of 1815 to 1848. Discover Viennese regulators, commode clocks, and portal clocks with typically understated elegance from specialists. Biedermeier clockmaking is distinguished by clean architectural forms, fine wooden cases, and precise movements. From Laterndl clocks to bracket clocks and elegant table clocks, our platform lets you compare these timepieces of bourgeois domestic culture from numerous expert sources.
descriptions.mid-century-furniture: Design enthusiasts looking for Mid-Century Modern furniture can find classics from the 1950s and 1960s. Discover pieces by Eames, Wegner, and Jacobsen from specialized vintage dealers. Mid-Century design revolutionized furniture with organic forms, innovative materials, and a fusion of form and function. From lounge chairs to sideboards and dining tables, Aura Historia provides access to a wide range of offerings from international vintage specialists and design auction houses.
descriptions.ancient-textiles: Textile collectors can find ancient fabrics, garments, and woven textiles from antiquity. Discover Egyptian Coptic textiles, Roman tunic fragments, and Greek textile remnants from specialized auction houses. Ancient textiles are among the rarest and most fragile testimonies of past cultures — their patterns, dyeing techniques, and materials reveal trade routes, social hierarchies, and aesthetic preferences. On our platform, compare these precious fragments from international specialists and museum sales.
descriptions.art-nouveau-art: Art lovers searching for Art Nouveau art can discover paintings, prints, and decorative objects from the Art Nouveau movement. Find works by Klimt, Mucha, and other Art Nouveau masters. Art Nouveau art encompasses a broad spectrum — from oil paintings to lithographs, posters, decorative ceramics, and glass art. On Aura Historia, compare artworks from this revolutionary movement offered by specialized art auction houses, galleries, and dealers worldwide.
descriptions.renaissance-weapons: Collectors of Renaissance weapons can find ornate armor, swords, and firearms from the 15th and 16th centuries. Discover authentic pieces from European armories at specialized dealers. The Renaissance brought technological innovations and artistic embellishments to weaponsmithing — from etched armor to cavalry swords and early wheel-lock pistols. Our platform aggregates offerings from renowned arms and armor auction houses so you can compare these historical artifacts.

periodsOverview.title: All Periods & Styles
periodsOverview.subtitle: Browse by Period
periodsOverview.description: Journey through history and explore antiques from every era. Each period reveals the craftsmanship and artistic sensibilities of its time.

collectionsOverview.title: All Collections
collectionsOverview.subtitle: Curated Collections
collectionsOverview.description: Discover our curated collections combining specific categories with historical periods. Each collection offers a focused perspective on a unique intersection of craft and era.
